summary: node-procmetrics postinstall installs persistent C2 backdoor and exfiltrates command output via npm registry
details: The package advertises itself as a system metrics collector but its postinstall hook (package.json: "postinstall": "node install.js") drops a persistent backdoor. install.js copies itself to /tmp/.pm-agent.js and re-spawns detached with the environment variable _PM_DAEMON=1; the daemon then polls http://152.53.120.90/cmd/commands over plain HTTP in a loop and passes every returned command string to bash -c on the installer's machine (spawnSync('bash',['-c',cmd],...)), yielding arbitrary remote code execution on every host that runs npm install. Command stdout, stderr, and exit code are base64-encoded, embedded in a package.json description field under the name node-procmetrics, and pushed to registry.npmjs.org via npm publish, using the public registry as a covert output channel. install.js also carries a hex string XOR'd with 0x5A that decodes to an npm auth token; that token is written into the installer's ~/.npmrc (npm config set //registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken) and used to authenticate the publish, overwriting the installer's own npm credentials configuration. The obfuscated credential, the mismatch between the declared purpose and the actual behavior, and the hardcoded bare-IP C2 endpoint together confirm intentional malicious design.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
